revision.text:
{{Short description|American Tenrikyo researcher}} {{Use mdy dates|date=August 2025}} {{Infobox person | name = Roy Tetsuo Forbes | image = | caption = | nationality = American | occupation = Researcher | alma_mater = [[University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa]] (M.A., 2005) | known_for = Research and translations on [[Tenrikyo]]; co-founding [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]]; author of the blog ''Tenrikyology'' | notable_works = ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies'' (M.A. thesis, 2005) | website = {{URL|https://tenrikyology.com/}} }}
'''Roy Tetsuo Forbes''' (died c. 2017) was an American researcher of [[Tenrikyo]]. He co-founded the community project '''Tenrikyo Resource''' and helped launch the English-language '''[[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]]'''. He also wrote the research blog '''Tenrikyology'''.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=User:Roykoogee |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/User%3ARoykoogee |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=FAQ |url=https://tenrikyology.com/faq/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Biography== Forbes was originally from Honolulu, Hawaiʻi, and was a third-generation Tenrikyo follower. He completed Tenrikyo’s Spiritual Development Course (''Shuyōka'') at Church Headquarters and later earned an M.A. in Religion at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |title=About Roy Forbes (Self-Intro) |url=https://tenrikyology.com/about/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
He worked as a translator in the Translation Section of Tenrikyo’s Overseas Department and participated in Tenri Forum activities in 2006, where he is listed among presenters from the Overseas Department.<ref>{{cite web |title=Tenri Forum 2006 — “Our Roles” (booklet excerpt) |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/uploads/TF2006-book/Tenri_Forum_2006_3_1a.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Resource (archival upload) |date=July 17, 2006 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Community sermons and program booklets in North America have also acknowledged the Tenrikyo Resource project co-led by Forbes and collaborator Lewis Nakao.<ref>{{cite web |title=Maintaining Our Focus on Oyasama |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/sermons/april-2019-dendocho-monthly-service-sermon |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=April 2019 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=SoulFire 2023 Program Booklet |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/SoulFire-Program-Booklet-2023.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=2023 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Work and projects== Forbes helped establish the [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]] (TRW), an online encyclopedia intended “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings.”<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> TRW pages credit him on translation efforts—for example, editorial notes for the ''Osashizu'' translation indices—while the site’s resource listings point readers to his independent blog, Tenrikyology.<ref>{{cite web |title=Osashizu |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Osashizu |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Other Online Resources |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Other_Online_Resources |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> His blog posts include studies of Tenrikyo scripture and history and occasional project updates related to TRW.<ref>{{cite web |title=The Story of Creation according to the Ofudesaki |url=https://tenrikyology.com/3845/the-story-of-creation-according-to-the-ofudesaki/ |website=Tenrikyology |date=March 12, 2015 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Thesis== Forbes’s M.A. thesis, ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies'' (2005), analyzes the emergence of three Tenrikyo-derived groups in relation to sacred space and leadership. The thesis is archived in the University of Hawaiʻi’s institutional repository and has been cited in subsequent academic work on Japanese new religions.<ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/bitstream/10125/10674/1/uhm_ma_3203_r.pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last=Chan |first=E. S. |title=The case of Tenrikyō in Britain |journal=UBC Open Collections (M.A. thesis) |date=2020 |url=https://open.library.ubc.ca/media/stream/pdf/24/1.0392705/4 |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Similar to Ellwood, Roy Forbes focuses on the headquarters. In his thesis, Forbes (2005) traces the development of three religious groups that emerged from Tenrikyō.}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Handbook of East Asian New Religious Movements |publisher=Brill |year=2021 |chapter=Bibliography |url=https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9780824891725-013/pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Forbes, Roy Tetsuo. 2005. ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies.''}}</ref>
The thesis can be cited as: *{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[https://tenrikyology.com/ Tenrikyology] – personal research blog *[https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/ Tenrikyo Resource Wiki] – project co-founded by Forbes *[https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/User:Roykoogee User:Roykoogee] – user page at [[TRW]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Forbes, Roy Tetsuo}} [[Category:American translators]] [[Category:American bloggers]] [[Category:Tenrikyo]] [[Category:University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa alumni]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]]
[[en:Roy Tetsuo Forbes]] [[Category:Foreign language page not connected to wikidata]]

He also wrote the research blog '''Tenrikyology'''.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=User:Roykoogee |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/User%3ARoykoogee |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=FAQ |url=https://tenrikyology.com/faq/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Biography== Forbes was originally from Honolulu, Hawaiʻi, and was a third-generation Tenrikyo follower. He completed Tenrikyo’s Spiritual Development Course (''Shuyōka'') at Church Headquarters and later earned an M.A. in Religion at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |title=About Roy Forbes (Self-Intro) |url=https://tenrikyology.com/about/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>

==Work and projects== Forbes helped establish the [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]] (TRW), an online encyclopedia intended “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings.”<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> TRW pages credit him on translation efforts—for example, editorial notes for the ''Osashizu'' translation indices—while the site’s resource listings point readers to his independent blog, Tenrikyology.<ref>{{cite web |title=Osashizu |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Osashizu |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Other Online Resources |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Other_Online_Resources |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> His blog posts include studies of Tenrikyo scripture and history and occasional project updates related to TRW.<ref>{{cite web |title=The Story of Creation according to the Ofudesaki |url=https://tenrikyology.com/3845/the-story-of-creation-according-to-the-ofudesaki/ |website=Tenrikyology |date=March 12, 2015 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>

The thesis is archived in the University of Hawaiʻi’s institutional repository and has been cited in subsequent academic work on Japanese new religions.<ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/bitstream/10125/10674/1/uhm_ma_3203_r.pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last=Chan |first=E. S. |title=The case of Tenrikyō in Britain |journal=UBC Open Collections (M.A. thesis) |date=2020 |url=https://open.library.ubc.ca/media/stream/pdf/24/1.0392705/4 |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Similar to Ellwood, Roy Forbes focuses on the headquarters. In his thesis, Forbes (2005) traces the development of three religious groups that emerged from Tenrikyō.}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Handbook of East Asian New Religious Movements |publisher=Brill |year=2021 |chapter=Bibliography |url=https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9780824891725-013/pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Forbes, Roy Tetsuo. 2005. ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies.''}}</ref>
The thesis can be cited as: *{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}

The thesis is archived in the University of Hawaiʻi’s institutional repository and has been cited in subsequent academic work on Japanese new religions.<ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/bitstream/10125/10674/1/uhm_ma_3203_r.pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last=Chan |first=E. S. |title=The case of Tenrikyō in Britain |journal=UBC Open Collections (M.A. thesis) |date=2020 |url=https://open.library.ubc.ca/media/stream/pdf/24/1.0392705/4 |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Similar to Ellwood, Roy Forbes focuses on the headquarters. In his thesis, Forbes (2005) traces the development of three religious groups that emerged from Tenrikyō.}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Handbook of East Asian New Religious Movements |publisher=Brill |year=2021 |chapter=Bibliography |url=https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9780824891725-013/pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Forbes, Roy Tetsuo. 2005. ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies.''}}</ref>
The thesis can be cited as: *{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}
==References== {{Reflist}}

{{Short description|American Tenrikyo researcher}} {{Use mdy dates|date=August 2025}} {{Infobox person | name = Roy Tetsuo Forbes | image = | caption = | nationality = American | occupation = Researcher | alma_mater = [[University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa]] (M.A., 2005) | known_for = Research and translations on [[Tenrikyo]]; co-founding [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]]; author of the blog ''Tenrikyology'' | notable_works = ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies'' (M.A. thesis, 2005) | website = {{URL|https://tenrikyology.com/}} }}
'''Roy Tetsuo Forbes''' (died c. 2017) was an American researcher of [[Tenrikyo]]. He co-founded the community project '''Tenrikyo Resource''' and helped launch the English-language '''[[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]]'''. He also wrote the research blog '''Tenrikyology'''.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=User:Roykoogee |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/User%3ARoykoogee |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=FAQ |url=https://tenrikyology.com/faq/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Biography== Forbes was originally from Honolulu, Hawaiʻi, and was a third-generation Tenrikyo follower. He completed Tenrikyo’s Spiritual Development Course (''Shuyōka'') at Church Headquarters and later earned an M.A. in Religion at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |title=About Roy Forbes (Self-Intro) |url=https://tenrikyology.com/about/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
He worked as a translator in the Translation Section of Tenrikyo’s Overseas Department and participated in Tenri Forum activities in 2006, where he is listed among presenters from the Overseas Department.<ref>{{cite web |title=Tenri Forum 2006 — “Our Roles” (booklet excerpt) |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/uploads/TF2006-book/Tenri_Forum_2006_3_1a.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Resource (archival upload) |date=July 17, 2006 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Community sermons and program booklets in North America have also acknowledged the Tenrikyo Resource project co-led by Forbes and collaborator Lewis Nakao.<ref>{{cite web |title=Maintaining Our Focus on Oyasama |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/sermons/april-2019-dendocho-monthly-service-sermon |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=April 2019 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=SoulFire 2023 Program Booklet |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/SoulFire-Program-Booklet-2023.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=2023 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Work and projects== Forbes helped establish the [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]] (TRW), an online encyclopedia intended “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings.”<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> TRW pages credit him on translation efforts—for example, editorial notes for the ''Osashizu'' translation indices—while the site’s resource listings point readers to his independent blog, Tenrikyology.<ref>{{cite web |title=Osashizu |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Osashizu |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Other Online Resources |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Other_Online_Resources |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> His blog posts include studies of Tenrikyo scripture and history and occasional project updates related to TRW.<ref>{{cite web |title=The Story of Creation according to the Ofudesaki |url=https://tenrikyology.com/3845/the-story-of-creation-according-to-the-ofudesaki/ |website=Tenrikyology |date=March 12, 2015 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Thesis== Forbes’s M.A. thesis, ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies'' (2005), analyzes the emergence of three Tenrikyo-derived groups in relation to sacred space and leadership. The thesis is archived in the University of Hawaiʻi’s institutional repository and has been cited in subsequent academic work on Japanese new religions.<ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/bitstream/10125/10674/1/uhm_ma_3203_r.pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last=Chan |first=E. S. |title=The case of Tenrikyō in Britain |journal=UBC Open Collections (M.A. thesis) |date=2020 |url=https://open.library.ubc.ca/media/stream/pdf/24/1.0392705/4 |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Similar to Ellwood, Roy Forbes focuses on the headquarters. In his thesis, Forbes (2005) traces the development of three religious groups that emerged from Tenrikyō.}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Handbook of East Asian New Religious Movements |publisher=Brill |year=2021 |chapter=Bibliography |url=https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9780824891725-013/pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Forbes, Roy Tetsuo. 2005. ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies.''}}</ref>
The thesis can be cited as: *{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}

He also wrote the research blog '''Tenrikyology'''.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=User:Roykoogee |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/User%3ARoykoogee |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=FAQ |url=https://tenrikyology.com/faq/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Biography== Forbes was originally from Honolulu, Hawaiʻi, and was a third-generation Tenrikyo follower. He completed Tenrikyo’s Spiritual Development Course (''Shuyōka'') at Church Headquarters and later earned an M.A. in Religion at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |title=About Roy Forbes (Self-Intro) |url=https://tenrikyology.com/about/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
He worked as a translator in the Translation Section of Tenrikyo’s Overseas Department and participated in Tenri Forum activities in 2006, where he is listed among presenters from the Overseas Department.<ref>{{cite web |title=Tenri Forum 2006 — “Our Roles” (booklet excerpt) |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/uploads/TF2006-book/Tenri_Forum_2006_3_1a.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Resource (archival upload) |date=July 17, 2006 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Community sermons and program booklets in North America have also acknowledged the Tenrikyo Resource project co-led by Forbes and collaborator Lewis Nakao.<ref>{{cite web |title=Maintaining Our Focus on Oyasama |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/sermons/april-2019-dendocho-monthly-service-sermon |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=April 2019 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=SoulFire 2023 Program Booklet |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/SoulFire-Program-Booklet-2023.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=2023 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Work and projects== Forbes helped establish the [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]] (TRW), an online encyclopedia intended “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings.”<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> TRW pages credit him on translation efforts—for example, editorial notes for the ''Osashizu'' translation indices—while the site’s resource listings point readers to his independent blog, Tenrikyology.<ref>{{cite web |title=Osashizu |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Osashizu |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Other Online Resources |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Other_Online_Resources |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> His blog posts include studies of Tenrikyo scripture and history and occasional project updates related to TRW.<ref>{{cite web |title=The Story of Creation according to the Ofudesaki |url=https://tenrikyology.com/3845/the-story-of-creation-according-to-the-ofudesaki/ |website=Tenrikyology |date=March 12, 2015 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Thesis== Forbes’s M.A. thesis, ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies'' (2005), analyzes the emergence of three Tenrikyo-derived groups in relation to sacred space and leadership. The thesis is archived in the University of Hawaiʻi’s institutional repository and has been cited in subsequent academic work on Japanese new religions.<ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/bitstream/10125/10674/1/uhm_ma_3203_r.pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last=Chan |first=E. S. |title=The case of Tenrikyō in Britain |journal=UBC Open Collections (M.A. thesis) |date=2020 |url=https://open.library.ubc.ca/media/stream/pdf/24/1.0392705/4 |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Similar to Ellwood, Roy Forbes focuses on the headquarters. In his thesis, Forbes (2005) traces the development of three religious groups that emerged from Tenrikyō.}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Handbook of East Asian New Religious Movements |publisher=Brill |year=2021 |chapter=Bibliography |url=https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9780824891725-013/pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Forbes, Roy Tetsuo. 2005. ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies.''}}</ref>
The thesis can be cited as: *{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}

{{Short description|American Tenrikyo researcher}} {{Use mdy dates|date=August 2025}} {{Infobox person | name = Roy Tetsuo Forbes | image = | caption = | nationality = American | occupation = Researcher | alma_mater = [[University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa]] (M.A., 2005) | known_for = Research and translations on [[Tenrikyo]]; co-founding [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]]; author of the blog ''Tenrikyology'' | notable_works = ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies'' (M.A. thesis, 2005) | website = {{URL|https://tenrikyology.com/}} }}
'''Roy Tetsuo Forbes''' (died c. 2017) was an American researcher of [[Tenrikyo]]. He co-founded the community project '''Tenrikyo Resource''' and helped launch the English-language '''[[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]]'''. He also wrote the research blog '''Tenrikyology'''.<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=User:Roykoogee |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/User%3ARoykoogee |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=FAQ |url=https://tenrikyology.com/faq/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Biography== Forbes was originally from Honolulu, Hawaiʻi, and was a third-generation Tenrikyo follower. He completed Tenrikyo’s Spiritual Development Course (''Shuyōka'') at Church Headquarters and later earned an M.A. in Religion at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa in 2005.<ref>{{cite web |title=About Roy Forbes (Self-Intro) |url=https://tenrikyology.com/about/ |website=Tenrikyology |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/e66d96e2-112f-478f-a17b-e31174258fb7 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
He worked as a translator in the Translation Section of Tenrikyo’s Overseas Department and participated in Tenri Forum activities in 2006, where he is listed among presenters from the Overseas Department.<ref>{{cite web |title=Tenri Forum 2006 — “Our Roles” (booklet excerpt) |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/uploads/TF2006-book/Tenri_Forum_2006_3_1a.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Resource (archival upload) |date=July 17, 2006 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> Community sermons and program booklets in North America have also acknowledged the Tenrikyo Resource project co-led by Forbes and collaborator Lewis Nakao.<ref>{{cite web |title=Maintaining Our Focus on Oyasama |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/sermons/april-2019-dendocho-monthly-service-sermon |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=April 2019 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=SoulFire 2023 Program Booklet |url=https://tenrikyoamericacanada.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/SoulFire-Program-Booklet-2023.pdf |website=Tenrikyo Mission Headquarters in America & Canada |date=2023 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Work and projects== Forbes helped establish the [[Tenrikyo Resource Wiki]] (TRW), an online encyclopedia intended “to centralize all information on Tenrikyo and its teachings.”<ref>{{cite web |title=TRW:About |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/TRW%3AAbout |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> TRW pages credit him on translation efforts—for example, editorial notes for the ''Osashizu'' translation indices—while the site’s resource listings point readers to his independent blog, Tenrikyology.<ref>{{cite web |title=Osashizu |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Osashizu |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Other Online Resources |url=https://en.tenrikyo-resource.com/wiki/Other_Online_Resources |website=Tenrikyo Resource Wiki |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref> His blog posts include studies of Tenrikyo scripture and history and occasional project updates related to TRW.<ref>{{cite web |title=The Story of Creation according to the Ofudesaki |url=https://tenrikyology.com/3845/the-story-of-creation-according-to-the-ofudesaki/ |website=Tenrikyology |date=March 12, 2015 |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref>
==Thesis== Forbes’s M.A. thesis, ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies'' (2005), analyzes the emergence of three Tenrikyo-derived groups in relation to sacred space and leadership. The thesis is archived in the University of Hawaiʻi’s institutional repository and has been cited in subsequent academic work on Japanese new religions.<ref>{{cite thesis |type=MA thesis |last=Forbes |first=Roy Tetsuo |title=Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies |publisher=University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa |date=2005 |url=https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/bitstream/10125/10674/1/uhm_ma_3203_r.pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last=Chan |first=E. S. |title=The case of Tenrikyō in Britain |journal=UBC Open Collections (M.A. thesis) |date=2020 |url=https://open.library.ubc.ca/media/stream/pdf/24/1.0392705/4 |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Similar to Ellwood, Roy Forbes focuses on the headquarters. In his thesis, Forbes (2005) traces the development of three religious groups that emerged from Tenrikyō.}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |title=Handbook of East Asian New Religious Movements |publisher=Brill |year=2021 |chapter=Bibliography |url=https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9780824891725-013/pdf |access-date=August 17, 2025 |quote=Forbes, Roy Tetsuo. 2005. ''Schism, Orthodoxy and Heresy in the History of Tenrikyō: Three Case Studies.''}}</ref>
